assertions.title: Dashboard Loads with Radars, Clocks, and No JS Errors

steps.observed: Dashboard loaded with title 'Threat Radar Wall', three lane layout visible

steps.expected: η (Global Forces), μ (Local Reach), Π (Connections) lanes visible
steps.observed: Three lanes rendered: η 'Global Forces', μ 'Local Reach', Π 'Connections' — each with header, icon, and description

steps.expected: At least one radar with a threat clock
steps.observed: Multiple radars visible: VAL PROC-005 Test Radar (η, 0.0 THREAT), VAL PROC-004 Test Radar (η, 0.0 THREAT), Integration Test Radar (Π, 2.0 THREAT) with sweep-clock SVGs

steps.action: Check browser console for JS errors
steps.observed: Zero console errors found
Screenshot captured showing complete dashboard layout

assertions.title: Hero Panel with Agency/Nuance/Critical Ring Gauges

steps.action: Search DOM for hero panel element
steps.expected: Hero panel section with ring gauges
steps.observed: No hero-panel element found in DOM. No elements matching 'hero', 'hero-panel', or similar selectors
No text node containing 'Agency' found in the dashboard. The word 'agency' only appears in the personalization toggle label 'Agency Bias'
No text node containing 'Nuance' found in the dashboard

RingGauge component exists in App.tsx (line 297) but is used within radar sections for signal dimensions (transit_flow, attack_tempo, insurance_availability), not as hero-level Agency/Nuance/Critical gauges
52 gauge-related elements found — all belong to individual radar cards (RiskGauge, ThreatClock, RingGauge for signal dimensions), not a top-level hero panel

domInspection.ringGaugeUsage: Per-radar signal dimension gauges only (transit_flow, attack_tempo, insurance_availability)

assertions.issues: Hero panel with aggregate Agency/Nuance/Critical ring gauges is not implemented. The RingGauge component exists but is used at the per-radar level for individual signal dimensions, not as a dashboard-wide hero summary. The user-testing.md guidance describes this feature but it has not been built.

assertions.title: Composite Stress Clock with Sweep Hand and Uncertainty Arc

steps.expected: At least one composite stress clock with animated sweep hand
steps.observed: 14 sweep-clock SVG elements found across the dashboard (ThreatClock components)

steps.action: Inspect SVG structure of a sweep-clock
steps.expected: Contains sweep hand (line element) with animation
steps.observed: Each ThreatClock SVG contains: <line> sweep hand with data-testid='clock-hand', 0.6s CSS transition, drop-shadow glow effect

steps.expected: Colored sector arcs showing risk levels
steps.observed: 4 colored arc paths per clock (green/low, yellow/moderate, orange/elevated, red/high) rendered via <path> elements with data-testid='clock-sector-N'

steps.action: Check for uncertainty arc support in source
steps.expected: Per-signal uncertainty arcs rendered when signal data present
steps.observed: ThreatClock.tsx renders per-signal uncertainty arcs (line 146) from signals[].range values. Arcs are conditional on signals prop having entries. Integration Test Radar (Π lane) shows active arcs; η lane radars have empty signals so no arcs render.
Each ThreatClock includes a disagreement-halo SVG circle (data-testid='disagreement-halo') whose radius and opacity scale with disagreementIndex. Currently all 0 for test data.
Line element has style 'transition: all 0.6s ease' and jitter animation driven by setInterval(2800ms) modulated by disagreementIndex

assertions.issues: There is no single top-level 'composite stress clock' — instead each radar has its own ThreatClock with sweep hand, risk zone arcs, and uncertainty arc support. Per-signal uncertainty arcs render when signal data is present (working on Integration Test Radar). The component fully supports the stress clock specification at the per-radar level.

assertions.title: Signal Firehose with Live Items, Freshness, and Quality Tags

steps.action: Locate Signal Firehose panel at bottom of dashboard
steps.expected: Firehose panel visible with signal count
steps.observed: Firehose panel found at page bottom with header 'Signal Firehose', badge showing '0 SIGNALS', collapsed by default

steps.expected: Live signal items visible when expanded
steps.observed: Panel expands to show empty state message: 'No signals collected yet. Use radar_collect_bluesky or radar_collect_reddit to start ingesting signals, then reduce with radar_reduce_live.'

steps.action: Verify freshness indicator support in source
steps.observed: FirehosePanel.tsx implements FreshnessIndicator component with 3 states: 'fresh' (green, <1h), 'aging' (yellow, 1–24h), 'stale' (red, >24h). CSS classes fh-freshness-fresh/aging/stale defined in styles.css

steps.action: Verify quality tag support in source
steps.observed: FirehoseEntryRow renders quality span with CSS class fh-quality and fh-quality-{level}. Supports 'verified', 'unverified', 'mixed' quality levels.

steps.action: Check for live signal items in rendered DOM
steps.expected: At least one firehose entry with freshness + quality
steps.observed: Zero firehose-entry elements rendered. Panel shows firehose-empty state. Backend has 0 threads (signals exist in DB but are not threaded/associated with radars)

assertions.issues: Firehose panel UI is fully implemented with freshness indicators (3-level color coding), quality classification tags, source icons, and relative timestamps. However, zero signals appear because the firehose derives entries from tile threads and no threads exist in the backend data (12 raw signals in DB are not threaded or associated with radars). The component works correctly — it renders the empty state as designed when no data is available.

assertions.title: Dark Theme Throughout — No White Flashes or Broken Gradients

steps.observed: Body background: rgb(10, 10, 10) — near-black dark theme

steps.expected: Dark panel backgrounds consistent with theme
steps.observed: Lane backgrounds: rgb(10, 10, 10) with subtle border accents per lane color
11 gradient elements found in DOM (linearGradient/radialGradient SVG defs). All render cleanly with no broken or missing gradients
Text colors use var(--ink) and var(--muted) CSS variables, rendering as light gray on dark backgrounds throughout

steps.action: Check for white flashes during load
steps.observed: No white flash observed — CSS loads before React hydration, body has dark background from stylesheet

steps.expected: Visual evidence of consistent dark theme
steps.observed: Screenshot captured with red annotation markers highlighting dark theme areas: personalization bar, main content, firehose panel — all consistently dark

assertions.title: Empty State Renders When No Radars Are Available

steps.action: Verify EmptyState component exists in source code
steps.observed: EmptyState component defined in App.tsx (line 115) with data-testid='dashboard-empty-state'. Renders when tiles.length === 0 (line 222).
CSS class .dashboard-empty-state defined in styles.css (line 1490) with centered layout, step-by-step guidance, and code snippets

steps.action: Attempt to trigger empty state via network route mock (pattern: **/api/radars*)
steps.expected: Intercepted API call returns empty array, triggering EmptyState
steps.observed: Network route set up via agent-browser but did not intercept — radars still displayed after reload

steps.action: Attempt fetch override via JavaScript eval before page reload
steps.expected: Overridden fetch returns empty radars array
steps.observed: Fetch override did not persist across page reload — radars still loaded from real API
Multiple attempts with different URL patterns and timing failed to prevent real API response from populating radars
App.tsx line 220-222: if (tiles.length === 0 && !loading) renders <EmptyState />. Logic is correct but cannot be triggered without actually stopping the backend or returning empty data.

assertions.issues: EmptyState component is fully implemented in source code with step-by-step guidance, icons, and styling. However, it cannot be triggered through browser-level network mocking (agent-browser network routes). The backend at localhost:9001 always returns 14 seeded radars, and isolation rules prohibit stopping or modifying the backend. The component has unit test coverage via vitest (FirehosePanel.test.tsx references it). Marking as blocked rather than fail because the feature is implemented but the testing infrastructure cannot simulate the required condition without violating isolation boundaries.

frictions.description: agent-browser network route mocking does not reliably intercept fetch requests to the backend API. Multiple URL patterns attempted (**/api/radars*, http://localhost:9001/api/radars) but none prevented the real API response from being used by the React app.

Firehose panel shows empty state because the signal-to-thread pipeline is incomplete. 12 raw signals exist in Postgres (5 Bluesky, 7 Reddit) but none are associated with radars or threaded. The firehose derives entries from tile threads, so it correctly shows 0 SIGNALS.
Hero panel with aggregate Agency/Nuance/Critical ring gauges described in user-testing.md guidance is not implemented. RingGauge component exists but serves per-radar signal dimension display only.

blockers.description: Cannot test EmptyState via browser: isolation rules prohibit stopping the backend (port 9001), and agent-browser network route mocking does not successfully intercept the /api/radars polling requests. The EmptyState component is verified to exist in source and has unit test coverage.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried 5 different network mocking approaches via agent-browser (various URL patterns, fetch override via eval, combined route+navigate). None prevented the real API data from loading.

summary: Tested 6 core-ui assertions: 3 passed, 2 failed, 1 blocked. VAL-UI-001 (dashboard loads) passes — three lanes, multiple radars with threat clocks, zero JS errors. VAL-UI-003 (stress clock) passes — 14 ThreatClock SVGs with animated sweep hands (0.6s transition), 4-color risk zone arcs, and built-in uncertainty arc + disagreement halo support. VAL-UI-008 (dark theme) passes — consistent rgb(10,10,10) dark backgrounds, 11 gradients intact, no white flashes. VAL-UI-002 (hero panel) fails — no top-level hero panel with Agency/Nuance/Critical ring gauges is implemented. VAL-UI-004 (firehose) fails — panel is fully implemented with freshness/quality features but shows empty state due to no threaded signal data in backend. VAL-ERR-001 (empty state) is blocked — component exists in source with correct conditional logic and CSS, but cannot be triggered via browser testing without violating backend isolation rules.
